jungle.136: From Up High enables tower dives and vertical plays, though it lags at 44.3% winrate.

offlane.134: Prosperous Path provides kill-based gold or healing at 46.9% winrate; take if expecting frequent kills.
offlane.135: Masterful Mimicry dominates at 50.7% winrate; extra clones excel in extended teamfights and lane pressure.
offlane.136: From Up High scales height damage but underperforms at 48.0% winrate; avoid unless enemy lacks vertical counterplay.

jungle.137: Energy Transfer's 53.3% winrate provides the shield and mobility needed to initiate or escape ganks safely in jungle skirmishes.
jungle.138: Pick Storm of Salvation for sustained dueling and skirmishes where your 51.5% winrate healing converts extended fights into advantages.
jungle.139: Serpent's Blessing enables rapid terrain-based repositioning with its 50% cooldown reduction, but Energy Transfer's 53.3% winrate offers better protection.

offlane.137: Energy Transfer's 51.3% winrate shield helps survive poke damage, though Storm of Salvation's 52.0% winrate is generally superior.
offlane.138: Storm of Salvation's 52.0% winrate sustains you through prolonged lane trades and teamfights where healing stacking matters most.
offlane.139: Serpent's Blessing offers 50.6% winrate terrain repositioning, but avoid it unless your offlane has frequent natural terrain angles.

jungle.122: Take Soul Weaver for sustained teamfights where you'll hit multiple heroes, stacking damage reduction to enable aggressive plays.
jungle.123: Pick Hallowed Reverberation to extend your gank range and clear speed with bouncing strikes across the map.

offlane.122: Soul Weaver's 50.4% winrate makes it reliable for tanking poke and teamfight damage in the sidelane.
offlane.123: Hallowed Reverberation's 52.9% winrate dominates offlane; extended range and bounces control space against multiple enemies.
offlane.124: Avoid Ephemeral Surge unless prioritizing mana efficiency; Soul Weaver and Hallowed Reverberation outperform it significantly offlane.

offlane.76: Take The Ascended to scale into late game as offlane's primary tank, with 49.4% winrate proving its reliability over 142,443 games.

jungle.74: Flow State's 49.0% winrate suits aggressive junglers who chain Evade resets through frequent takedowns and skirmishes.
jungle.75: Master Of Spears barely justifies jungle play at 48.0% winrate; prioritize The Ascended or Flow State instead.
jungle.76: The Ascended dominates jungle at 52.4% winrate with consistent stat scaling to duel and gank effectively across the map.

support.140: Pick when enemies group tight; bouncing 25% further and 10% cooldown reduction per hero hit enables sustained poke.
support.141: Choose when your team needs sustained healing despite 15% less per charge; dual charges enable more frequent top-ups.
support.142: Take this as primary choice; 49.2% winrate shows Terminal Treatment excels at pressuring low-health targets in fights.

offlane.140: Pick for extended duels; ricochet mechanics help pressure multiple opponents while managing cooldowns through repeated hits.
offlane.141: Avoid; only 78 games with 39.7% winrate suggest dual charges don't compensate for reduced healing in this role.
offlane.142: Prioritize this; 52.0% winrate demonstrates Terminal Treatment dominates offlane by punishing damaged enemies in skirmishes.

midlane.140: Pick against grouped enemies; bouncing further helps control midlane teamfights with consistent damage output.
midlane.141: Avoid; minimal 151 games at 45.7% suggest dual charges don't justify reduced healing for midlane's positioning demands.
midlane.142: Take this; 53.5% winrate shows Terminal Treatment's unmatched strength for finishing low-health targets in midlane fights.

carry.140: Pick when kiting; ricochet bounces help maintain damage while repositioning against grouped threats.
carry.141: Consider only if extreme sustain needed; 49.4% winrate on tiny sample suggests dual charges barely compete here.
carry.142: Prioritize this; 52.5% winrate confirms Terminal Treatment excels at eliminating weakened targets during carry fights.

support.Lotus: A permanent buff every 2 minutes and every takedown — Adele support roams for picks, and each one compounds; the field's clear favorite here.
support.Idrisil: 15% extra armors while healthy plus a break shield — lets Adele frontline her engages instead of just starting them.
support.Krix: Flat max-health growth for a melee support who lives in the scrum — the safest pick, and the role's biggest sample.

offlane.Lotus: Compounding stats every 2 minutes suit a lane that drags into the late game; takedowns in long trades stack it faster.
offlane.Krix: 5% (+1% per level) max health is honest offlane durability — the lane's most-trusted pick by volume.
offlane.Nihil: Doom rewards staying on a target, which offlane trades allow — but the sample here is thin; treat as situational.

jungle.Lotus: Every camp clear ticks the 2-minute clock and every gank can add a takedown proc — compounding stats fit a farming assassin.

midlane.Demiurge: 12% more from her AP core — sim and field both have it playable, a step behind.

support.Lotus: Compounding stats on support income — the field's best result in the role.
support.Exarch: Her Verdant Embrace healing (with that augment) can trigger Exarch's gift — niche pairing, thin sample above even.
support.Aion: Mana per cast for zone spam — right at even.

carry.Lotus: Permanent buffs compound toward his late dual-wield windows — narrowly the carry leader in a tough set.
carry.Thraex: The duel mark pays sustained target focus — just under even on volume.
carry.Xyris: Farm-fed damage growth — similar result; nothing dominates on him.

midlane.Thraex: Mark-and-shoot between ability cycles — leads a close midlane sample.
midlane.Vermis: Every 3rd basic max-health chunk — his attack speed sustains the cadence; near even.
midlane.Demiurge: Our sim's biggest number (12% item amplification) — field has all three near even; take the math if you trust it.

carry.Lotus: Compounding buffs lead a carry sample where his results all hover under even.
carry.Aion: Mana per cast funds his sniper poke — similar, slightly behind.
carry.Demiurge: Our sim's standout number on his item scaling — the field's bigger sample disagrees; weight the field until it turns.

midlane.Lotus: Permanent 2-minute buffs on a scaling sniper — the lane's evidence leader.
midlane.Demiurge: 12% item amplification — huge sim number, big sample under even; the disagreement itself is the takeaway.

support.Lotus: Compounding stats on support income suit a utility sniper — the field's best result.
support.Demiurge: Item amplification arrives too late on support gold — right at even.
support.Aion: Cast-return mana for poke — just under even.

jungle.Demiurge: 12% more item stats on his stat-hungry clone kit — narrowly the jungle leader.
jungle.Lotus: Compounding buffs, effectively tied — both clear the rest.
jungle.Xyris: Camp-fed growth — under even; the clone farm doesn't cash it.

offlane.Demiurge: Item amplification leads the offlane sample — his clones inherit the bigger stats.
offlane.Lotus: The compounding alternative — thin sample, just under even.
offlane.Xyris: Farm growth — under even on a small sample.

jungle.Lotus: Takedown procs plus the 2-minute clock on a snowballing duelist — an exceptional result, with Demiurge right behind.
jungle.Demiurge: 12% more from her stat-stick core — nearly tied for the lead.
jungle.Thraex: Mark-and-lash single-target — her big sample holds far above even; all three are strong.

offlane.Thraex: Offlane duels with a marked target are Yin's whole game — the lane's clear leader.
offlane.Xyris: Waveclear-fed growth — above even on a modest sample.
offlane.Demiurge: Item amplification — close behind; she wins with all of these.

jungle.Lotus: Compounding permanent stats on a scaling spirit jungler — the field's clear pick.
jungle.Demiurge: 12% more item stats — our modeled number is solid, the field sample sits under even.

offlane.Demiurge: Item amplification narrowly leads a thin offlane sample — backed by our sim's modeled gain.
offlane.Lotus: The compounding pick, effectively tied on an equally thin sample.
offlane.Aion: Mana per cast for her wave spam — right at even.

offlane.Lotus: Permanent buffs through a long bruiser lane — the only pick above even in this set.
offlane.Krix: Max health on a sustained brawler — under even here.
offlane.Thraex: The duel mark's big sample is clearly losing — the field has moved on from it on Zarus.

jungle.Lotus: Takedown procs from ganks compound on the 2-minute clock — a strong jungle result.
jungle.Demiurge: 12% more from his bruiser core — right at even.
jungle.Krix: Plain max health — under even; he wants stats that fight back.

support.Lotus: Compounding buffs on support income — narrowly the role's best result.
support.Thraex: Her whip-crack autos keep the mark applied — just under even on good volume.
support.Vesh: Our sim's modeled pick for her ability chip — field sits under even; the math assumes more casting than support allows.

offlane.Thraex: Offlane Zinx duels with autos and the mark pays both directions — a clear evidence leader.
offlane.Xyris: Farm-fed growth — thin sample, above even.
offlane.Lotus: The compounding fallback — thin sample, above even.

midlane.Thraex: Mark-and-poke with her long-range autos — leads the lane on a real sample.
midlane.Lotus: Compounding buffs, close behind on a thin sample.
midlane.Demiurge: 12% item amplification — above even; her hybrid scaling uses all of it.

carry.Thraex: Carry Zinx keeps one target marked all fight — the only qualifying sample in the role, and it's above even.
